title: Southern California Association of Disability Examiners (SCADE) — Constitution and By-Laws
description: Combined Constitution and By-Laws of the Southern California Association of Disability Examiners (SCADE), a California chapter of the National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) covering members in the San Diego, Los Angeles East, and Los Angeles West state-agency service areas. The Constitution covers name, purpose, geographic composition, membership, the Executive Board (President/President-elect/Secretary/Treasurer plus the immediate past president, Monthly Update editor, and membership director), meetings, amendments, limitations, and adoption. The By-Laws define the disability examiner, eight categories of full membership plus associate membership, general meetings, elections, Executive Board powers, officer duties, and committees.
geographic_coverage: California — Southern California (San Diego, Los Angeles East, Los Angeles West service areas)

source.publisher: National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) — Southern California Chapter
source.title: Constitution and By-Laws of the Southern California Association of Disability Examiners (SCADE)

notes: Authoritative NADE chapter governing document. This .md is the markitdown-normalized conversion of the sibling source file 'Cal-South-Const-Bylaws.docx' in the same directory; expect conversion artifacts (body rendered bold; the by-laws heading reads 'SOUTHERN CALIFONIA'). Source-text inconsistency: the Constitution's Article 1 abbreviates the chapter as 'SACADE' while the By-Laws (Article 1) define the abbreviation as 'SCADE'; the canonical chapter acronym throughout the By-Laws is SCADE, used here. No adoption/revision date is stated (the document references a June 30, 1977 grandfather clause for legacy full members but states no overall adoption date), so temporal_coverage is omitted. The source URL is the NADE organization site, not a deep link.
